title: fix: preserve Vertex upstream error metadata
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Preserve Vertex AI response status, body, and endpoint through the image error funnel.
- Populate Tinybird error records with upstream host/status/body instead of `undefined`.
- Add a regression test for a Vertex 502 response.

Root cause: Vertex failures were wrapped in a plain `Error`, discarding HTTP metadata before tracking.

Tests: `npx vitest run test/image/vertexAIClient.test.ts test/image/vertexAIImageGenerator.test.ts test/image/contentModeration.test.ts`

gist.headline: Vertex keeps the receipts
gist.blurb: Vertex image errors now arrive with their useful little labels still attached. The hive can see the upstream status, response body, and endpoint instead of a mysterious blank stare.
gist.summary: Preserved Vertex AI HTTP status, response body, and endpoint metadata through the image error funnel so Tinybird records upstream host/status/body for failures such as 502 responses.
gist.impact: Vertex image-generation failures now carry actionable upstream diagnostics, helping the team identify and resolve provider-side issues faster.
gist.visual_concept: A cozy garden workshop where Polly helps the eager CRT Monitor Robot sort clearly labeled Vertex error parcels: a 502 tag, a tiny response scroll, and an endpoint signpost. Code-plants and flowers represent observability growing from previously blank error records.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene in a warm garden hackerspace: the main character is a cute pixel art bee mascot with a yellow body and dark brown horizontal stripes, small black legs, two black antennae, black vertical oval eyes, bright pink rectangular blush marks on cheeks, a small happy smile, and small rounded light blue translucent wings; she wears tiny detective glasses and helps a cute chunky CRT-monitor robot file labeled error parcels beside a retro terminal showing a clear "502" tag, response scroll, and endpoint signpost, with code-plants blooming nearby. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel gradient background, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes like Stardew Valley or A Short Hike, dark purple (#110518) outlines, nostalgic but beautiful and emotionally warm.
